jobs.description:
As a Data Manager within Global Manufacturing and Supply (GMS) you will be the key person to build and manage the processes, governance and system landscape for our data. In other words, you shape the vision of our future data management set up and will be the driver of the data journey within GMS.
 In our view, good data needs to enable correct and on time supplies to patients and create positive business impact. You will be accountable that all key data of respective systems (e.g. ERP, APS) are created timely, are correct, maintained, synchronized among systems, and communicated to internal and external stakeholders at a high standard and that data quality is reported regularly to stakeholders.
 You will also partner with functions outside GMS and participate in projects that aim to enhance Sobi’s data management environment.
 This position is based at our Global Headquarters in Stockholm, Sweden or Global hub in Basel, Switzerland, reporting directly to Head Global Manufacturing & Supply Excellence.
 Key Responsibilities 
 
• Define, implement, and further develop cross-functional processes and RACI (incl. governance) to create and update Master Data.

• Manage the identification, collection, consolidation and cleansing of master data from various sources.

• Create and update data points and ensure that data sharing among systems works uninterrupted.

• Ensure accurate and timely communication of master data to e.g. 3PL partners and/or Sobi affiliates.

• Define and map source system and user systems of each data point.

• Develop and coordinate / implement data integration strategies to ensure data consistency across systems.

• Collaborate well with various stakeholders within and outside GMS to define and implement ways of working.

• Develop and implement concepts to regularly report data quality, data sharing, and next improvement steps.

• Proactively initiate and potentially lead improvements on processes, systems and roles & responsibilities to enhance data management within GMS, TechOps and Sobi. E.g. Collaborate with IT and software teams to design and implement data solutions including data models, data structures and data interfaces.

• Visualize data points, working processes and systems to business impact (Sobi-TechOps-GMS strategy/value creation plan) and explain / train stakeholders on a) the importance of accurate and timely data management and b) how to collaborate in an efficient and effective way to forester a data-driven culture.

• Be accountable for data related chang controls, respective SOP updates and training / guiding documentation as applicable and set an efficient governance for updating documentation.

• Define a framework for self-inspection on data accuracy to ensure continuous data quality improvement.
 

 About you 
 You are a collaborative, patient-focused professional who thrives in a global, multicultural environment. You enjoy connecting the dots between functions, translating complexity of data into clear direction, and building alignment among diverse stakeholders. You model Sobi’s values through your positive mindset, flexibility, and willingness to go the extra mile to support our mission.
 Your experience in similar roles allows you to navigate complexity with confidence and deliver results that help ensure continued access to treatments for rare disease communities.
 Key Qualifications 
 
• 5 – 10 years of experience in data management, Information Technology, and supply chain functions.

• Minimum 5 years’ GMP/GDP experience in the pharmaceutical or biotech industry.

• Experience working effectively in intercultural and highly matrixed environments.

• Proven ability to build, influence and embed data strategies / process re-designs across cross-functional and remote teams.

• University degree in Data Analytics, Computer science, Information systems, Supply Chain Management, or a related field.

• Strong presentation and communication skills.

• Excellent written and verbal English.

• Strong proficiency in MS Office, ERP systems, Advanced Planning Systems (APS) and Data Management Tools.
 Nice to Have 
 
• Experience in Data analytics and system implementation

• Project management qualification

• Lean Six Sigma qualification / experience

• Knowledge on IFS and Kinaxis Maestro

• AI experience

• Additional langue skills such as Swedish or German
 Here at Sobi we live by our core values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership!

 How to apply 
 We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible. Click apply and include your resume.
 Why Join Us? 
 Here at Sobi, our mission and culture get us excited to come to work every day, but here are a few more reasons to join our team:
 
• Collaborative and team-oriented environment

• Opportunities for professional growth

• Diversity and Inclusion

• Competitive compensation for your work

• Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments

• Emphasis on work/life balance
  
 Sobi is a global company with over 1,900 employees in more than 30 countries and are committed to the societies where we operate. We are a specialised international biopharmaceutical company transforming the lives of people with rare and debilitating diseases, providing reliable access to innovative medicines in the areas of haematology, immunology, and specialty care. Sobi’s share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. More about Sobi at sobi.com and LinkedIn.

We know our employees are our most valuable asset and our culture conveys that. We offer a competitive benefits package, to support the health and happiness of our staff.

 Sobi Culture 

At Sobi, we refuse to accept the status quo. This is because we have witnessed first-hand the challenges facing those affected by rare diseases and have used this knowledge to shape our business to find new ways of helping them.

As a specialized biopharmaceutical company, we are dedicated to rare diseases. And we see this focus as a strength. By effectively turning our research into ground-breaking treatments, we help make medicine more accessible and open more possibilities for patients and more opportunities for those caring for them. This has been our approach since day one, but we know we can’t change the world of rare diseases on our own. Accomplishing this requires strong partnerships with patients, partners and stakeholders across the entire value chain. Together, we define how our business can create solutions that serve the needs of those affected by rare diseases while facilitating sustainable growth.

descriptionByLocale.it:
In qualità di Data Manager all'interno di Global Manufacturing and Supply (GMS) sarai la persona chiave per costruire e gestire i processi, la governance e il panorama del sistema per i nostri dati. In altre parole, formi la visione della nostra futura impostazione di gestione dei dati e sarai il motore del percorso dei dati all'interno del GMS. A nostro avviso, i buoni dati devono consentire forniture corrette e puntuali ai pazienti e creare un impatto positivo sul business. Sarai responsabile del fatto che tutti i dati chiave dei rispettivi sistemi (ad es. ERP, APS) siano creati tempestivamente, siano corretti, mantenuti, sincronizzati tra i sistemi e comunicati alle parti interessate interne ed esterne con uno standard elevato e che la qualità dei dati sia riferita regolarmente alle parti interessate. Collaborerai anche con funzioni esterne a GMS e parteciperai a progetti che mirano a migliorare l'ambiente di gestione dei dati di Sobi. Questa posizione ha sede presso la nostra sede centrale globale a Stoccolma, in Svezia, o presso l'hub globale di Basilea, in Svizzera, a diretto riporto di Head Global Manufacturing & Supply Excellence. Responsabilità principali
• Definire, implementare e sviluppare ulteriormente processi interfunzionali e RACI (inclusa la governance) per creare e aggiornare i dati master.
• Gestire l'identificazione, la raccolta, il consolidamento e la pulizia dei dati master da varie fonti.
• Creare e aggiornare i punti dati e garantire che la condivisione dei dati tra i sistemi funzioni ininterrottamente.
• Garantire una comunicazione accurata e tempestiva dei dati anagrafici, ad esempio, ai partner 3PL e/o alle affiliate Sobi.

• Ambiente collaborativo e orientato al team
• Opportunità di crescita professionale
• Diversità e inclusione
• Compenso competitivo per il tuo lavoro
• Avere un impatto positivo per aiutare i pazienti affetti da malattie ultra rare che hanno bisogno di trattamenti salvavita
• Enfasi sull'equilibrio tra lavoro e vita privata Sobi è un'azienda globale con oltre 1.900 dipendenti in più di 30 paesi e si impegna nelle società in cui operiamo. Siamo un'azienda biofarmaceutica internazionale specializzata che trasforma la vita delle persone affette da malattie rare e debilitanti, fornendo un accesso affidabile a farmaci innovativi nei settori dell'ematologia, dell'immunologia e delle cure specialistiche. La quota di Sobi è quotata al Nasdaq di Stoccolma. Maggiori informazioni su Sobi su sobi.com e LinkedIn. Sappiamo che i nostri dipendenti sono il nostro bene più prezioso e la nostra cultura lo trasmette. Offriamo un pacchetto di vantaggi competitivi, per sostenere la salute e la felicità del nostro personale. Cultura Sobi In Sobi, ci rifiutiamo di accettare lo status quo. Questo perché abbiamo assistito in prima persona alle sfide che devono affrontare le persone affette da malattie rare e abbiamo utilizzato queste conoscenze per plasmare la nostra attività e trovare nuovi modi per aiutarle. descriptionByLocale.de:
Als Datenmanager bei Global Manufacturing and Supply (GMS) sind Sie die Schlüsselperson für den Aufbau und die Verwaltung der Prozesse, der Governance und der Systemlandschaft für unsere Daten. Mit anderen Worten, Sie gestalten die Vision unseres zukünftigen Datenmanagements und werden der Treiber der Datenreise innerhalb von GMS sein. Aus unserer Sicht müssen gute Daten eine korrekte und pünktliche Versorgung der Patienten ermöglichen und positive Auswirkungen auf das Geschäft haben. Sie sind dafür verantwortlich, dass alle Schlüsseldaten der jeweiligen Systeme (z. B. ERP, APS) zeitnah erstellt, korrekt, gepflegt, zwischen den Systemen synchronisiert und an interne und externe Stakeholder auf hohem Niveau kommuniziert werden und dass die Datenqualität regelmäßig an die Stakeholder gemeldet wird. Sie werden auch mit Funktionen außerhalb von GMS zusammenarbeiten und an Projekten teilnehmen, die darauf abzielen, die Datenverwaltungsumgebung von Sobi zu verbessern. Diese Position hat ihren Sitz in unserem globalen Hauptsitz in Stockholm, Schweden, oder Global Hub in Basel, Schweiz, und berichtet direkt an Head Global Manufacturing & Supply Excellence. Schlüsselverantwortlichkeiten
• Definition, Implementierung und Weiterentwicklung funktionsübergreifender Prozesse und RACI (inkl. Governance) zur Erstellung und Aktualisierung von Stammdaten.
• Verwalten der Identifizierung, Sammlung, Konsolidierung und Bereinigung von Stammdaten aus verschiedenen Quellen.
• Erstellen und aktualisieren Sie Datenpunkte und stellen Sie sicher, dass der Datenaustausch zwischen den Systemen ununterbrochen funktioniert.
• Sicherstellung einer genauen und zeitnahen Übermittlung von Stammdaten an z. B. 3PL-Partner und/oder Sobi-Tochtergesellschaften.
• Definieren und zuordnen Hier bei Sobi begeistern uns unsere Mission und Kultur, jeden Tag zur Arbeit zu kommen, aber hier sind ein paar weitere Gründe, sich unserem Team anzuschließen:
• Kollaboratives und teamorientiertes Umfeld
• Möglichkeiten für berufliches Wachstum
• Vielfalt und Inklusion
• Wettbewerbsfähige Vergütung für Ihre Arbeit
• Einen positiven Einfluss haben, um Patienten mit extrem seltenen Krankheiten zu helfen, die lebensrettende Behandlungen benötigen
• Schwerpunkt auf Work-Life-Balance Sobi ist ein globales Unternehmen mit über 1.900 Mitarbeitern in mehr als 30 Ländern und engagiert sich für die Gesellschaften, in denen wir tätig sind. Wir sind ein spezialisiertes internationales biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen, das das Leben von Menschen mit seltenen und schwächenden Krankheiten verändert und einen zuverlässigen Zugang zu innovativen Medikamenten in den Bereichen Hämatologie, Immunologie und Spezialversorgung bietet. Sobis Aktie ist an der Nasdaq Stockholm notiert. Mehr über Sobi unter sobi.com und LinkedIn. Wir wissen, dass unsere Mitarbeiter unser wertvollstes Gut sind, und unsere Kultur vermittelt dies. Wir bieten ein wettbewerbsfähiges Leistungspaket, um die Gesundheit und das Glück unserer Mitarbeiter zu unterstützen. Sobi-Kultur Bei Sobi weigern wir uns, den Status quo zu akzeptieren. Dies liegt daran, dass wir die Herausforderungen für von seltenen Krankheiten betroffene Menschen aus erster Hand miterlebt und dieses Wissen genutzt haben, um unser Geschäft zu gestalten und neue Wege zu finden, um ihnen zu helfen. Als spezialisiertes biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen widmen wir uns seltenen Krankheiten. Und wir sehen diesen Fokus als Stärke. Indem wir unsere Forschung effektiv in bahnbrechende Behandlungen umwandeln, können wir
descriptionByLocale.fr:
En tant que gestionnaire de données au sein de Global Manufacturing and Supply (GMS), vous serez la personne clé pour construire et gérer les processus, la gouvernance et le paysage système pour nos données. En d'autres termes, vous façonnez la vision de notre future configuration de gestion des données et serez le moteur du voyage des données au sein de GMS. À notre avis, de bonnes données doivent permettre de fournir des fournitures correctes et ponctuelles aux patients et de créer un impact commercial positif. Vous serez responsable que toutes les données clés des systèmes respectifs (par exemple ERP, APS) sont créées en temps opportun, sont correctes, maintenues, synchronisées entre les systèmes, et communiquées aux parties prenantes internes et externes à un niveau élevé et que la qualité des données est signalée régulièrement aux parties prenantes. Vous travaillerez également en partenariat avec des fonctions extérieures à GMS et participerez à des projets visant à améliorer l'environnement de gestion des données de Sobi. Ce poste est basé à notre siège mondial à Stockholm, en Suède, ou à notre centre mondial à Bâle, en Suisse, sous la responsabilité directe de Head Global Manufacturing & Supply Excellence. Responsabilités clés
• Définir, mettre en œuvre et développer davantage les processus interfonctionnels et RACI (y compris la gouvernance) pour créer et mettre à jour les données de base.
• Gérer l'identification, la collecte, la consolidation et le nettoyage des données de base provenant de diverses sources.
• Créer et mettre à jour des points de données et s'assurer que le partage des données entre les systèmes fonctionne sans interruption.
• Assurer une communication précise et en temps opportun des données de base, par exemple aux partenaires de 3PL et/ou aux filiales de Sobi.

L'action de Sobi est cotée au Nasdaq Stockholm. En savoir plus sur Sobi sur sobi.com et LinkedIn. Nous savons que nos employés sont notre atout le plus précieux et notre culture le transmet. Nous offrons un ensemble d'avantages sociaux compétitifs, pour soutenir la santé et le bonheur de notre personnel. Culture Sobi Chez Sobi, nous refusons d'accepter le statu quo. jobs.title: QA Analytical Specialist
jobs.description:
## Descrizione
About the role 
Global Quality plays a key role in making sure our medicines meet internal standards, regulatory expectations, and the commitments in our registration files. As an independent quality organisation, we bring the expertise to deliver on these commitments—while continuously strengthening our capabilities, developing our people, and evolving our quality system to support the business as we grow.
As QA Analytical Specialist, you’ll be the quality partner for analytical activities and stability studies for products where Sobi is the MAH. You’ll ensure GMP-compliant methods and data support reliable release and stability testing for both clinical and commercial supply—working closely with internal stakeholders and external partners to keep quality, compliance, and patient impact at the centre of everything we do.
It is a Hybrid role and can be based in either Stockholm or Basel, reporting to Head of Quality Product Lifecycle & Clinical Supply.

## Mansioni
- Coordinator and Quality approver for deviations, CAPA and change controls.
- QA-approver for GMP-related analytical activities, e.g. validation and technology transfer of analytical methods used for in-process control, release and stability studies.
- Performance of quality evaluation of CLOs.
- QA contact for CLOs
- Provide Quality Assurance for stability studies for products where Sobi is the MAH, including approving plans and protocols.
- Perform QA review and approval of extension of expiry date of critical analytical material, including but not limited to reference standards and control samples.
- Perform QA review and approval of product specifications.
- Give support to Global Regulatory Affairs / CMC in analytical quality issues for variations and market expansions.
- Act as a representative of Global Quality and provide QA analytical guidance to support projects in order to ensure compliance with GMP
- Manage Quality Technical Agreements with CLOs.
- Author, review and approve SOPs and related documentation related to the position.

## Requisiti
About you 
You’re a quality-minded analytical professional who enjoys working cross-functionally, taking ownership, and bringing structure to complex scientific work. You combine strong GMP understanding with a collaborative mindset, and you’re motivated by the opportunity to help deliver reliable, high-quality medicines to patients who need them most.

Qualifications 
- University degree in Chemistry, Biology, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Science, or similar.
- 5+ years’ experience in GMP analytical work within pharma/biotech—covering analytical method development and validation, product specifications, deviation investigations, and change control; ideally in QA supporting QC (or as a QC Coordinator/similar).
- Broad knowledge of analytical chemistry and bioassays, including clear and compliant documentation practices.
- Professional proficiency in English, written and spoken.
- You live the Sobi CARE values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership.
- Ability to work independently, take initiative, and make sound quality decisions.
- Strong communication skills and a collaborative, team-oriented approach.
- A structured mindset with attention to detail and a commitment to continuous improvement.

## Cosa offriamo
Here at Sobi we live by our core values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership!

Why Join Us? 
Here at Sobi, our mission and culture get us excited to come to work every day, but here are a few more reasons to join our team:
- Collaborative and team-oriented environment
- Opportunities for professional growth
- Diversity and Inclusion
- Competitive compensation for your work
- Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments
- Emphasis on work/life balance

Sobi is a global company with over 1,900 employees in more than 30 countries and are committed to the societies where we operate. We are a specialised international biopharmaceutical company transforming the lives of people with rare and debilitating diseases, providing reliable access to innovative medicines in the areas of haematology, immunology, and specialty care. Sobi’s share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. More about Sobi at sobi.com and LinkedIn.
We know our employees are our most valuable asset and our culture conveys that. We offer a competitive benefits package, to support the health and happiness of our staff.

## Sobi Culture 
At Sobi, we refuse to accept the status quo. This is because we have witnessed first-hand the challenges facing those affected by rare diseases and have used this knowledge to shape our business to find new ways of helping them.
As a specialized biopharmaceutical company, we are dedicated to rare diseases. And we see this focus as a strength. By effectively turning our research into ground-breaking treatments, we help make medicine more accessible and open more possibilities for patients and more opportunities for those caring for them. This has been our approach since day one, but we know we can’t change the world of rare diseases on our own. Accomplishing this requires strong partnerships with patients, partners and stakeholders across the entire value chain. Together, we define how our business can create solutions that serve the needs of those affected by rare diseases while facilitating sustainable growth.

## Contatto
How to apply 
We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible. Click apply and include your resume.

As QA Analytical Specialist, you’ll be the quality partner for analytical activities and stability studies for products where Sobi is the MAH. You’ll ensure GMP-compliant methods and data support reliable release and stability testing for both clinical and commercial supply—working closely with internal stakeholders and external partners to keep quality, compliance, and patient impact at the centre of everything we do.
It is a Hybrid role and can be based in either Stockholm or Basel, reporting to Head of Quality Product Lifecycle & Clinical Supply.

Mansioni
- Coordinator and Quality approver for deviations, CAPA and change controls.
- QA-approver for GMP-related analytical activities, e.g. validation and technology transfer of analytical methods used for in-process control, release and stability studies.
- Performance of quality evaluation of CLOs.
- QA contact for CLOs
- Provide Quality Assurance for stability studies for products where Sobi is the MAH, including approving plans and protocols.
- Perform QA review and approval of extension of expiry date of critical analytical material, including but not limited to reference standards and control samples.
- Perform QA review and approval of product specifications.
- Give support to Global Regulatory Affairs / CMC in analytical quality issues for variations and market expansions.
- Act as a representative of Global Quality and provide QA analytical guidance to support projects in order to ensure compliance with GMP
- Manage Quality Technical Agreements with CLOs.
- Author, review and approve SOPs and related documentation related to the position.

Requisiti
About you
You’re a quality-minded analytical professional who enjoys working cross-functionally, taking ownership, and bringing structure to complex scientific work. You combine strong GMP understanding with a collaborative mindset, and you’re motivated by the opportunity to help deliver reliable, high-quality medicines to patients who need them most.

Qualifications
- University degree in Chemistry, Biology, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Science, or similar.
- 5+ years’ experience in GMP analytical work within pharma/biotech—covering analytical method development and validation, product specifications, deviation investigations, and change control; ideally in QA supporting QC (or as a QC Coordinator/similar).
- Broad knowledge of analytical chemistry and bioassays, including clear and compliant documentation practices.
- Professional proficiency in English, written and spoken.
- You live the Sobi CARE values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership.
- Ability to work independently, take initiative, and make sound quality decisions.
- Strong communication skills and a collaborative, team-oriented approach.
- A structured mindset with attention to detail and a commitment to continuous improvement.

Cosa offriamo
Here at Sobi we live by our core values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership!

Why Join Us?
Here at Sobi, our mission and culture get us excited to come to work every day, but here are a few more reasons to join our team:
- Collaborative and team-oriented environment
- Opportunities for professional growth
- Diversity and Inclusion
- Competitive compensation for your work
- Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments
- Emphasis on work/life balance

Sobi is a global company with over 1,900 employees in more than 30 countries and are committed to the societies where we operate. We are a specialised international biopharmaceutical company transforming the lives of people with rare and debilitating diseases, providing reliable access to innovative medicines in the areas of haematology, immunology, and specialty care. Sobi’s share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. More about Sobi at sobi.com and LinkedIn.
We know our employees are our most valuable asset and our culture conveys that. We offer a competitive benefits package, to support the health and happiness of our staff.

Sobi Culture
At Sobi, we refuse to accept the status quo. This is because we have witnessed first-hand the challenges facing those affected by rare diseases and have used this knowledge to shape our business to find new ways of helping them.
As a specialized biopharmaceutical company, we are dedicated to rare diseases. And we see this focus as a strength. By effectively turning our research into ground-breaking treatments, we help make medicine more accessible and open more possibilities for patients and more opportunities for those caring for them. This has been our approach since day one, but we know we can’t change the world of rare diseases on our own. Accomplishing this requires strong partnerships with patients, partners and stakeholders across the entire value chain. Together, we define how our business can create solutions that serve the needs of those affected by rare diseases while facilitating sustainable growth.

Contatto
How to apply
We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible. Click apply and include your resume.

Come specialista analitico QA, sarete il partner di qualità per attività analitiche e studi di stabilità per i prodotti dove Sobi è il MAH. Garantirai metodi e test affidabili di rilascio e stabilità di GMP per l'offerta clinica e commerciale, lavorando a stretto contatto con gli stakeholder interni e i partner esterni per mantenere la qualità, la conformità e l'impatto dei pazienti al centro di tutto ciò che facciamo. Si tratta di un ruolo ibrido e può essere basato sia a Stoccolma che a Basilea, segnalando al responsabile del ciclo di vita del prodotto di qualità & alimentazione clinica. Qualifiche - Laurea universitaria in Chimica, Biologia, Biotecnologia, Scienza Farmaceutica, o simili. - 5+ anni di esperienza nel lavoro analitico GMP all’interno del farmaco/biotecnologia, che copre lo sviluppo e la validazione dei metodi analitici, le specifiche del prodotto, le indagini di deviazione e il controllo dei cambiamenti; idealmente nel QA che supporta QC (o come Coordinatore QC/similare). - Ampia conoscenza della chimica analitica e dei bioassai, comprese le pratiche di documentazione chiare e conformi. - Vivi i valori Sobi CARE: Cura, Ambizione, Urgenza, Proprietà e Partenariato. - Capacità di lavorare in modo indipendente, prendere iniziativa e prendere decisioni di qualità sana. - Forte capacità di comunicazione e un approccio collaborativo e orientato al team. - Una mentalità strutturata con attenzione ai dettagli e impegno al miglioramento continuo. Cosa? Qui a Sobi viviamo i nostri valori fondamentali: Cura, Ambizione, Urgenza, Proprietà e Partenariato! Perché unirsi a noi? Qui a Sobi, la nostra missione e la nostra cultura ci eccitano a lavorare ogni giorno, ma qui ci sono alcune altre ragioni per unirsi al nostro team: - Ambiente collaborativo e di team-oriented - Opportunità per la crescita professionale - Diversità e inclusione - Compenso competitivo per il vostro lavoro - Rendere un impatto positivo per aiutare i pazienti affetti da malattie ultra rare che hanno bisogno di trattamenti di salvataggio - l'enfasi sul lavoro/equilibrio di vita Sobi è una società globale impegnata con oltre 1900 persone. Siamo un'azienda biofarmaceutica internazionale specializzata che trasforma la vita di persone con malattie rare e debilitanti, fornendo un accesso affidabile a farmaci innovativi nelle aree di ematologia, immunologia e cura delle specialità. Questo perché abbiamo assistito in prima persona alle sfide che le persone colpite da malattie rare e abbiamo usato questa conoscenza per plasmare la nostra attività per trovare nuovi modi di aiutarli. Come azienda biofarmaceutica specializzata, siamo dedicati a malattie rare. E vediamo questo obiettivo come una forza. Trasformando efficacemente la nostra ricerca in trattamenti innovativi, aiutiamo a rendere la medicina più accessibile e aprire più possibilità per i pazienti e più opportunità per chi li cura. Questo è stato il nostro approccio dal primo giorno, ma sappiamo che non possiamo cambiare il mondo di malattie rare da soli. Il completamento di questo richiede forti partnership con pazienti, partner e stakeholder in tutta la catena del valore. Insieme, definiamo come il nostro business può creare soluzioni che servono le esigenze di coloro che sono colpiti da malattie rare, facilitando la crescita sostenibile. Beschreibung Über die Rolle Global Quality spielt eine wichtige Rolle, um sicherzustellen, dass unsere Medikamente interne Standards, regulatorische Erwartungen und die Verpflichtungen in unseren Registrierungsdateien erfüllen. Als unabhängige Qualitätsorganisation bringen wir das Know-how, um diese Verpflichtungen zu erfüllen – während wir unsere Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich stärken, unsere Menschen entwickeln und unser Qualitätssystem weiterentwickeln, um das Unternehmen bei unserem Wachstum zu unterstützen. Als QA Analytical Specialist sind Sie der Qualitätspartner für analytische Aktivitäten und Stabilitätsstudien für Produkte, in denen Sobi der MAH ist. Sie stellen sicher, dass GMP-konforme Methoden und Daten eine zuverlässige Freigabe- und Stabilitätsprüfung sowohl für die klinische als auch für die kommerzielle Versorgung unterstützen – eng mit internen Stakeholdern und externen Partnern zusammenarbeiten, um Qualität, Compliance und Patientenwirkung im Zentrum von allem zu halten, was wir tun. Es ist eine Hybrid-Rolle und kann in Stockholm oder Basel, berichtet an Leiter Qualitätsprodukt Lebenszyklus & Klinische Versorgung. Mansioni - Koordinator und Qualitätszulasser für Abweichungen, CAPA und Änderungskontrollen. - QA-Zulasser für GMP-bezogene analytische Aktivitäten, z.B. Validierung und Technologietransfer von Analysemethoden, die für In-Prozess-Kontroll-, Release- und Stabilitätsstudien verwendet werden. - Leistung der Qualitätsbewertung von CLOs. - QA-Kontakt für CLOs - Qualitätssicherung für Stabilitätsstudien für Produkte, bei denen Sobi der MAH ist, einschließlich Genehmigungspläne und Protokolle. - Durchführung der QA-Überprüfung und Genehmigung der Verlängerung des Ablaufs von kritischem analytischem Material, einschließlich aber nicht beschränkt auf Referenzstandards und Kontrollproben. - Führen Sie QA Überprüfung und Genehmigung der Produktspezifikationen durch. - Global Regulatory Affairs / CMC unterstützen in analytischen Qualitätsfragen für Variationen und Markterweiterungen. - Aktivieren Sie als Vertreter der Global Quality und liefern Sie QA analytische Beratung, um Projekte zu unterstützen, um die Einhaltung von GMP - Management Quality Technical Agreements mit CLOs zu gewährleisten. - Autor, Überprüfung und Genehmigung von SOPs und damit zusammenhängenden Dokumentationen im Zusammenhang mit der Position.

Sie kombinieren ein starkes GMP-Verstehen mit einer kollaborativen Denkweise, und Sie sind von der Möglichkeit motiviert, zuverlässige, qualitativ hochwertige Medikamente für Patienten zu liefern, die sie am meisten brauchen. 
• Qualifications - Universitätsabschluss in Chemie, Biologie, Biotechnologie, Pharmazie oder dergleichen. - 5+ Jahre Erfahrung in der GMP-Analysearbeit innerhalb von Pharma/Biotech – Erfassung der analytischen Methodenentwicklung und Validierung, Produktspezifikationen, Abweichungsuntersuchungen und Änderungskontrolle; idealerweise in QA unterstützenden QC (oder als QC-Koordinator/Similar). - breites Wissen über analytische Chemie und Bioassays, einschließlich klarer und konformer Dokumentationspraktiken. - Sie leben die Sobi CARE Werte: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership und Partnership. - Fähigkeit, unabhängig zu arbeiten, Initiative zu ergreifen und fundierte Qualitätsentscheidungen zu treffen. - Starke Kommunikationsfähigkeiten und ein kollaborativer, teamorientierter Ansatz. - Eine strukturierte Denkweise mit Liebe zum Detail und eine Verpflichtung zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung. 
• Cosa offriamo Hier bei Sobi leben wir von unseren Kernwerten: Pflege, Ambition, Not, Eigentum und Partnerschaft! Warum bei uns? Hier bei Sobi erregt unsere Mission und Kultur uns, jeden Tag zur Arbeit zu kommen, aber hier sind einige weitere Gründe, um unserem Team beizutreten: - Zusammenarbeit und teamorientierte Umgebung - Chancen für professionelles Wachstum - Diversity and Inclusion - Wettbewerbsausgleich für Ihre Arbeit - Einen positiven Einfluss auf ultra-rare Krankheit Patienten zu helfen, die lebensersparende Behandlungen benötigen - Emphasis on Work/life Balance Sobi ist weltweit a900 Mitarbeiter Wir sind ein spezialisiertes internationales biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen, das das Leben von Menschen mit seltenen und verheerenden Krankheiten transformiert und einen zuverlässigen Zugang zu innovativen Arzneimitteln in den Bereichen Hämatologie, Immunologie und Spezialpflege bietet. Sobis Anteil ist auf Nasdaq Stockholm aufgeführt. Mehr über Sobi bei sobi.com und LinkedIn. Wir wissen, dass unsere Mitarbeiter unser wertvollstes Gut sind und unsere Kultur das vermittelt. 
• Wir bieten ein wettbewerbsfähiges Leistungspaket, um die Gesundheit und das Glück unserer Mitarbeiter zu unterstützen. Sobi Culture Bei Sobi weigern wir uns, den Status quo zu akzeptieren. Das liegt daran, dass wir die Herausforderungen, denen seltene Krankheiten ausgesetzt sind, aus erster Hand erlebt haben und dieses Wissen genutzt haben, um unser Geschäft zu gestalten, um neue Wege zu finden, ihnen zu helfen. Als spezialisiertes biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen widmen wir uns seltenen Krankheiten. Und wir sehen diesen Fokus als Stärke. Indem wir unsere Forschung effektiv in bahnbrechende Behandlungen verwandeln, helfen wir, die Medizin zugänglicher zu machen und mehr Möglichkeiten für Patienten und mehr Möglichkeiten für die Pflege für sie zu eröffnen. Das ist unser Ansatz seit dem ersten Tag, aber wir wissen, dass wir die Welt der seltenen Krankheiten nicht allein verändern können. Dies erfordert starke Partnerschaften mit Patienten, Partnern und Stakeholdern in der gesamten Wertschöpfungskette. Gemeinsam definieren wir, wie unser Unternehmen Lösungen schaffen kann, die den Bedürfnissen der von seltenen Krankheiten betroffenen Menschen dienen und ein nachhaltiges Wachstum ermöglichen. Descrizione À propos du rôle de Global Quality joue un rôle clé pour s'assurer que nos médicaments répondent aux normes internes, aux attentes réglementaires et aux engagements dans nos dossiers d'enregistrement. En tant qu'organisation de qualité indépendante, nous apportons l'expertise nécessaire pour respecter ces engagements, tout en renforçant continuellement nos capacités, en développant notre personnel et en développant notre système de qualité pour soutenir l'entreprise en pleine croissance. En tant que spécialiste en analyse de l'AQ, vous serez le partenaire de qualité pour les activités analytiques et les études de stabilité pour les produits où Sobi est le MAH. Vous vous assurerez que les méthodes et les données conformes aux BPF soutiennent des essais fiables de libération et de stabilité pour l'approvisionnement clinique et commercial, en travaillant en étroite collaboration avec les intervenants internes et les partenaires externes pour maintenir la qualité, la conformité et l'impact patient au centre de tout ce que nous faisons. Il s'agit d'un rôle hybride et peut être basé à Stockholm ou Bâle, relevant du responsable du cycle de vie des produits de qualité et de l'approvisionnement clinique. Mansioni - Coordonnatrice et approbatrice de qualité pour les écarts, l'ACAP et les contrôles des changements. - AQ-approver pour les activités d'analyse liées aux BPF, par exemple la validation et le transfert de technologie des méthodes d'analyse utilisées pour les études de contrôle, de libération et de stabilité en cours de fabrication. - Réalisation de l'évaluation de la qualité des CLO. - Personne-ressource de l'AQ pour les ALC - Fournir l'assurance de la qualité pour les études de stabilité des produits où Sobi est le titulaire de l'AMM, y compris l'approbation des plans et des protocoles. - Procéder à l'examen de l'AQ et à l'approbation de la prolongation de la date d'expiration du matériel analytique essentiel, y compris, sans s'y limiter, les normes de référence et les échantillons témoins. - Examiner et approuver les spécifications du produit. - Appuyer les Affaires réglementaires mondiales / CMC dans les questions de qualité analytique pour les variations et l'expansion du marché. - Agir en tant que représentant de la qualité mondiale et fournir des conseils analytiques sur l'AQ pour appuyer les projets afin d'assurer le respect des BPF - Gérer les accords techniques de qualité avec les OPC. - Auteur, révise et approuve les PNE et la documentation connexe relative au poste. 
• Requisiti À propos de vous Vous êtes un professionnel analytique de qualité qui aime travailler de manière interfonctionnelle, s'approprier et structurer des travaux scientifiques complexes. Vous combinez une bonne compréhension des BPF avec un état d'esprit collaboratif, et vous êtes motivé par la possibilité d'aider à fournir des médicaments fiables et de haute qualité aux patients qui en ont le plus besoin. 
• Qualifications - Diplôme universitaire en chimie, en biologie, en biotechnologie, en sciences pharmaceutiques ou similaires. - 5+ ans d'expérience en travail d'analyse des BPF au sein de la pharmacologie et de la biotechnologie - couvrant la mise au point et la validation de méthodes d'analyse, les spécifications du produit, les enquêtes sur les écarts et le contrôle des changements; idéalement dans l'AQ appuyant le QC (ou comme coordonnateur/similaire du QC). - Connaissance approfondie de la chimie analytique et des essais biologiques, y compris des pratiques de documentation claires et conformes. - Compétence professionnelle en anglais, écrit et parlé. - Capacité de travailler de façon indépendante, de prendre des initiatives et de prendre des décisions judicieuses en matière de qualité. - De solides compétences en communication et une approche collaborative et axée sur l'équipe. - Un état d'esprit structuré avec une attention aux détails et un engagement à l'amélioration continue. 
• Cosa offriamo Ici, à Sobi, nous vivons par nos valeurs fondamentales : Soins, Ambition, Urgence, Propriété et Partenariat ! Pourquoi nous rejoindre ? Ici, à Sobi, notre mission et notre culture nous incitent à venir travailler tous les jours, mais voici quelques autres raisons de nous joindre à notre équipe : - Environnement collaboratif et axé sur l'équipe - Possibilités de croissance professionnelle - Diversité et inclusion - Compensation compétitive de votre travail - Apporter un impact positif pour aider les patients atteints de maladies ultra-rares qui ont besoin de traitements de sauvetage - Mettre l'accent sur l'équilibre travail/vie privée Sobi est une entreprise mondiale qui compte plus de 1 900 employés dans plus de 30 pays et qui s'engage dans les sociétés où nous opérons. Nous sommes une entreprise biopharmaceutique internationale spécialisée qui transforme la vie des personnes atteintes de maladies rares et débilitantes, fournissant un accès fiable aux médicaments innovants dans les domaines de l'hématologie, de l'immunologie et des soins spécialisés. Sa part est cotée sur Nasdaq Stockholm. Plus d'informations sur Sobi sur sobi.com et LinkedIn. Nous savons que nos employés sont notre atout le plus précieux et que notre culture le démontre. 
• Nous offrons un ensemble d'avantages concurrentiels, pour soutenir la santé et le bonheur de notre personnel. Sobi Culture Chez Sobi, nous refusons le statu quo. C'est parce que nous avons été témoins de première main des défis auxquels sont confrontés les personnes touchées par des maladies rares et que nous avons utilisé ces connaissances pour façonner notre entreprise afin de trouver de nouvelles façons de les aider. En tant qu'entreprise biopharmaceutique spécialisée, nous sommes dédiés aux maladies rares. Et nous voyons cette concentration comme une force. En transformant efficacement nos recherches en traitements révolutionnaires, nous aidons à rendre la médecine plus accessible et à ouvrir davantage de possibilités aux patients et à ceux qui s'en occupent. Cela a été notre approche depuis le premier jour, mais nous savons que nous ne pouvons pas changer le monde des maladies rares par nous-mêmes. Pour y parvenir, il faut établir des partenariats solides avec les patients, les partenaires et les intervenants dans toute la chaîne de valeur. Ensemble, nous définissons comment notre entreprise peut créer des solutions qui répondent aux besoins des personnes touchées par des maladies rares tout en facilitant une croissance durable. jobs.description:
Manufacturing Science and Technology  (MSAT) is part of the Global CMC, Science & Technology Department (GCSAT).
 GCSAT is responsible for optimization and life cycle management of Sobis clinical and commercial manufacturing processes from internal and external network. MSAT ensures compliant, reliable, cost effective and sustainable manufacturing of our products now and in future. The MSAT team holds the senior scientific and technical knowledge for the manufacturing of Sobis clinical and commercial products and is responsible for always keeping the internal and external manufacturing processes in a validated stage.
 Main responsibilities:
 
• Process owner of DS validation for Sobi products.

• Lead and execute MSAT projects such as manufacturing process and cost improvement as part of LCM.

• Participate in internal activities related to (external) manufacturing such as process changes, major/critical deviations, risk assessments and CAPAs.

• Issue, review and approve applicable internal and external SOP’s and manufacturing instructions.

• Review and supervise set up, execution and documentation of technical studies, qualifications and validations.

• Write and review applicable sections of the APR/PQR.

• Write and review applicable sections in registration files, variations and market expansion.

• Support in sourcing evaluation of new DS contract manufacturers and participation in developing supply and quality agreements.

• Lead the technical part of a product transfer, scale up activities, validations and oversees manufacturing of DS processes at external manufacturers for Sobi projects in clinical and commercial phase.

• Be aware of external biopharmaceutical manufacturing technology advancements, understand and introduce new technologies and how they may be applicable to process innovation, cost or yield improvement
 

 
• University education in Chemistry, Biology, Biotechnology, Pharmacy or similar.

• +5 years of experience of development and manufacturing of Drug Substance for clinical and commercial use

• Scientific and technical background of CMC development and manufacturing of Drug Substance.

• Recognized expert in drug substance manufacturing

• Demonstrable experience of working in cross functional team with ability to multi task, prioritize and be an effective decision maker

• Experience of regulatory guidelines, regulatory authorities communication and file documentation

• Fluent in English
 

 How to apply 
 We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible. Click apply and include your resume in English. 
 Why Join Us?

jobs.description:
The IT department, a part of IS/IT, are responsible for architecture, strategy, maintenance and support of Sobi information systems and IT infrastructure as clients, servers, network and other infrastructure equipment.
 The IT department consist of four teams – IT Operations, Infrastructure & Cyber security, Digital Workplace and Automation & Integration.All IT activities are performed in accordance with Sobi’s quality framework (GxP) and applicable regulatory and authority requirements. 
 Within this context, the Digital Workplace & AI function plays a key role in enabling secure, compliant, and scalable digital solutions across Sobi. Under the leadership of the Head of Digital Workplace & AI, the team is responsible for developing and advancing Sobi’s Digital Workplace, Microsoft 365 platform, and enterprise AI capabilities. This includes setting strategic direction, defining objectives, and delivering modern, AI-enabled tools that support collaboration, productivity, and innovation across the organization. 
 Main responsibilities:
 
• Develop and maintain enterprise-grade AI applications built on Azure OpenAI, including chat interfaces, document processing pipelines (RAG), and real-time collaboration features 

 
• Design and implement Azure cloud solutions leveraging Azure Cosmos DB, Azure Cognitive Search, Azure Blob Storage, Azure Document Intelligence, and Azure AI Search  

 
• Build and optimize full-stack applications using Next.js 14, React 18, TypeScript, and modern frontend/backend patterns (App Router, Server Components, Server Actions) 

• Optimize AI/LLM integrations, including prompt engineering, token management, streaming completions, and multi-model AI orchestration 

• Ensure code quality, security, and compliance through code reviews, testing, documentation, and adherence to Sobi's quality framework (GxP) 

• Work proactively with ServiceNow to resolve incoming bug reports and other issues

 

 
• 4+ years of professional experience working as a software developer

• React / Next JS / Typescript / Azure Knowledge

• Proven ability to work autonomously and take ownership of features from concept to deployment

• Hands-on experience with Azure cloud services (Cosmos DB, Azure OpenAI, Cognitive Search, Blob Storage)

• Experience building and maintaining full-stack applications in production environment

• AI / LLM / Agent experience

• Fluent in English (written and spoken)
 Ideally you bring also:  
 
• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience)

• Experience in healthcare, pharmaceutical, or regulated industries preferred
 

 How to apply 
 We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible. Click apply and include your resume in English. 
 Why Join Us? 
 Here at Sobi, our mission and culture get us excited to come to work every day, but here are a few more reasons to join our team:
 
• Emphasis on work/life balance

• Collaborative and team-oriented environment

• Opportunities for professional growth

• Diversity and Inclusion

• Competitive compensation for your work

• Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments
 We are a global company with over 1,800 employees in more than 30 countries and are committed to the societies where we operate. With a deeply skilled management team directing our day-to-day wins, and a Board with a stellar track record, we’re ready to take on the world’s diseases, ailments and adversity. Our people believe they have the power to make a positive impact in others’ lives because that’s exactly what we do here. If you’re seeking a career that taps into your talents in a way that makes the world a better, healthier place, we just may have a job for you.

We know our employees are our most valuable asset, and our culture conveys that. We offer a competitive benefits package, to support the health and happiness of our staff.

 Sobi Culture 

At Sobi, we refuse to accept the status quo. This is because we have witnessed first-hand the challenges facing those affected by rare diseases and have used this knowledge to shape our business to find new ways of helping them.

As a specialized biopharmaceutical company, we are dedicated to rare diseases. And we see this focus as a strength. By effectively turning our research into ground-breaking treatments, we help make medicine more accessible and open up more possibilities for patients and more opportunities for those caring for them. This has been our approach since day one, but we know we can’t change the world of rare diseases on our own. Accomplishing this requires strong partnerships with patients, partners and stakeholders across the entire value chain. Together, we define how our business can create solutions that serve the needs of those affected by rare diseases while facilitating sustainable growth.

Con un team di gestione profondamente qualificato che dirige le nostre vincite quotidiane, e un Board con un record di traccia stellare, siamo pronti a prendere le malattie del mondo, disturbi e avversità. Avec une équipe de gestion profondément qualifiée dirigeant nos victoires quotidiennes, et un conseil avec un bilan stellaire, nous sommes prêts à prendre sur le monde des maladies, des maladies et de l'adversité. Notre peuple croit qu'il a le pouvoir d'avoir un impact positif sur les autres, parce que c'est exactement ce que nous faisons ici. Si vous cherchez une carrière qui tire profit de vos talents d'une manière qui fait du monde un endroit meilleur et plus sain, nous pouvons juste avoir un emploi pour vous. Nous savons que nos employés sont notre atout le plus précieux, et notre culture le démontre. Nous offrons un ensemble d'avantages concurrentiels, pour soutenir la santé et le bonheur de notre personnel. Sobi Culture Chez Sobi, nous refusons le statu quo. C'est parce que nous avons été témoins de première main des défis auxquels sont confrontés les personnes touchées par des maladies rares et que nous avons utilisé ces connaissances pour façonner notre entreprise afin de trouver de nouvelles façons de les aider. En tant qu'entreprise biopharmaceutique spécialisée, nous sommes dédiés aux maladies rares. Et nous voyons cette concentration comme une force. En transformant efficacement nos recherches en traitements révolutionnaires, nous aidons à rendre la médecine plus accessible et à ouvrir davantage de possibilités pour les patients et plus d'opportunités pour ceux qui les prennent en charge. Cela a été notre approche depuis le premier jour, mais nous savons que nous ne pouvons pas changer le monde des maladies rares par nous-mêmes. Pour y parvenir, il faut établir des partenariats solides avec les patients, les partenaires et les intervenants dans toute la chaîne de valeur. Ensemble, nous définissons comment notre entreprise peut créer des solutions qui répondent aux besoins des personnes touchées par des maladies rares tout en facilitant une croissance durable.

jobs.description:
Introduction/Purpose 
 This position can be based in Europe. 
 The CEERJ Commercial Operations and Tryngolza Organizational Readiness role plays a central role in enabling the CEERJ commercial organisation to operate with clarity, focus, and execution discipline by establishing and running a regional Commercial Project Management Office (PMO) and by driving selected high-priority strategic and transformation initiatives.

The role exists to bring structure, transparency, prioritisation, and follow-through to CEERJ initiatives spanning Launch Excellence, Commercial Excellence, Business Development & Alliance Management, and Partner Markets. It supports the CEERJ Commercial Excellence & Strategy Execution Director in ensuring that priorities are clear across the platform, interdependencies are actively managed, and agreed actions are executed consistently.

A core and explicit accountability of the role is to lead and drive CEERJ Organizational Readiness for Tryngolza, acting as the regional PMO owner for the organisational and operational transformation required to be ready for Tryngolza. This includes working in close partnership with the Launch Excellence function and other CEERJ teams to ensure that ways of working, processes, governance, organisational set-up, and capabilities across functions are fit for purpose to support the product as part of the CEERJ portfolio.

In addition to PMO and organizational readiness responsibilities, the role provides flexible, hands-on support across the CEERJ Commercial Platform, including temporarily stepping in to support sub-functions in case of capacity or capability gaps (e.g. maternity leave cover).

This position is well suited for a mid-level, high-potential professional with consulting and analytical experience, strong execution capabilities, high empathy, and a motivation to learn and operate across multiple commercial areas in a complex, matrixed environment.
 Major/Key Responsibilities: 
 CEERJ Commercial PMO Ownership
 
• Establish, maintain, and continuously improve the CEERJ Commercial PMO framework

• Provide end-to-end oversight of CEERJ commercial transformation and execution initiatives

• Consolidate priorities, milestones, ownership, risks, and interdependencies across initiatives

• Enable clarity on sequencing, focus, and resource allocation across the commercial platform

• Drive disciplined follow-up on agreed actions, decisions, and commitments
 Tryngolza Organizational Readiness (Regional PMO Lead)
 
• Act as PMO owner for CEERJ Organizational Readiness for Tryngolza, with responsibility for coordinating and driving organisational preparedness across functions

• Design and manage a structured readiness framework addressing:
 o ways of working
 o processes and governance
 o organisational set-up and interfaces
 o capability and skill readiness
 
• Work in close partnership with Launch Excellence and other CEERJ functions to align readiness activities, timelines, and expectations

• Ensure shared understanding of roles, responsibilities, and cross-functional dependencies

• Identify organisational gaps, risks, and interdependencies; drive mitigation actions and timely escalation

• Track and communicate readiness progress through clear, executive-ready insights
 Executive Enablement & Strategic Follow-Through
 
• Act as a trusted execution partner to the CEERJ Commercial Excellence & Strategy Execution Director

• Support agenda focus, prioritisation, and follow-up on strategic and transformation priorities

• Prepare structured updates, dashboards, and decision-ready materials for leadership forums

• Translate complex inputs into clear implications and recommended actions
 Cross-Functional Coordination & Constructive Conflict Resolution
 
• Engage closely with CEERJ sub-functions to ensure shared understanding and alignment

• Surface misalignments or tensions early and support resolution through deep listening and perspective-taking

• Resolve conflicts by facilitating balanced trade-offs and pragmatic compromises

• Foster a collaborative, trust-based way of working across the platform
 Flexible Delivery & Capability Support
 
• Temporarily support sub-functions during capacity or capability gaps

• Provide hands-on analytical, project, or coordination support where it adds the greatest value

• Adapt scope and focus dynamically in line with CEERJ priorities
 Insights Discovery & Analytics
 
• Apply a data-driven, insight-oriented mindset to identify patterns, risks, and improvement opportunities

• Use analytics to support prioritisation, performance tracking, and decision-making

• Convert insights into practical, actionable recommendations
  

 Education/Learning Experience 
 
• University degree

• Prior consulting experience strongly preferred

• Pharmaceutical / biotech industry experience required

• Experience supporting or leading organisational transformation, operating model change, or complex cross-functional programs

• Experience with data analytics, insights generation, or performance management
 Skills/Knowledge 
 
• Strong analytical, structuring, and problem-solving skills

• Ability to synthesise complexity into clear narratives

• Strong stakeholder management and influencing capabilities

• Excellent execution discipline and follow-through
 Personal Attributes 
 
• Humble, empathetic, and an excellent listener

• Resolves conflict through deep understanding of multiple perspectives

• Curious, flexible, and motivated to learn across commercial areas

• High integrity, ownership mindset, and accountability
 Languages 
 
• Fluent English (written & spoken)
 Additional Comments 
 
• Willingness to flex across topics, projects, and functional areas

• Availability to travel as required
 

 All Sobi employees need to demonstrate behaviors in line with Sobi's core values: Care, Ambition, Urgency, Ownership and Partnership. Are you ready to be on the Sobi team? Come join a culture that empowers every person to be the person that makes a difference for rare disease.
 Why Join Us? 

We are a global company with over 1,900 employees in more than 30 countries and are committed to the societies where we operate. With a deeply skilled management team directing our day-to-day wins, and a Board with a stellar track record, we’re ready to take on the world’s diseases, ailments and adversity. Our people believe they have the power to make a positive impact in others’ lives because that’s exactly what we do here. If you’re seeking a career that taps into your talents in a way that makes the world a better, healthier place, we just may have a job for you.

We know our employees are our most valuable asset, and our culture conveys that. We offer a competitive benefits package, to support the health and happiness of our staff.

 Sobi Culture 

At Sobi, we refuse to accept the status quo. This is because we have witnessed first-hand the challenges facing those affected by rare diseases, and have used this knowledge to shape our business to find new ways of helping them.

As a specialized biopharmaceutical company, we are dedicated to rare diseases. And we see this focus as a strength. By effectively turning our research into ground-breaking treatments, we help make medicine more accessible and open up more possibilities for patients and more opportunities for those caring for them. This has been our approach since day one, but we know we can’t change the world of rare diseases on our own. Accomplishing this requires strong partnerships with patients, partners and stakeholders across the entire value chain. Together, we define how our business can create solutions that serve the needs of those affected by rare diseases while facilitating sustainable growth.
 An Equal Opportunity Employer 

Sobi is an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. All employment decisions at Sobi are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, national, social or ethnic origin, sex (including pregnancy), age, physical, mental or sensory disability, HIV Status, sexual orientation, gender identity, protected veterans and/or expression, marital, civil union or domestic partnership status, past or present military service, family medical history or genetic information, family or parental status, or any other status or protected groups by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate.
 Sobi is an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer. Disabled/Veterans. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access this website to apply for a vacancy as a result of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to talentacquisition@sobi.com.

descriptionByLocale.it:
Introduzione/Scopo Questa posizione può avere sede in Europa. Il ruolo delle operazioni commerciali del CEERJ e della preparazione organizzativa di Tryngolza svolge un ruolo centrale nel consentire all'organizzazione commerciale del CEERJ di operare con chiarezza, concentrazione e disciplina di esecuzione istituendo e gestendo un ufficio regionale di gestione dei progetti commerciali (PMO) e guidando selezionate iniziative strategiche e di trasformazione ad alta priorità. Il ruolo esiste per portare struttura, trasparenza, definizione delle priorità e follow-up alle iniziative CEERJ che spaziano dall'eccellenza di lancio, all'eccellenza commerciale, allo sviluppo aziendale e alla gestione delle alleanze e ai mercati dei partner. Supporta il Direttore dell'eccellenza commerciale e dell'esecuzione della strategia del CEERJ nel garantire che le priorità siano chiare in tutta la piattaforma, le interdipendenze siano gestite attivamente e le azioni concordate siano eseguite in modo coerente. Una responsabilità fondamentale ed esplicita del ruolo è quella di guidare e guidare la prontezza organizzativa del CEERJ per Tryngolza, agendo come proprietario del PMO regionale per la trasformazione organizzativa e operativa necessaria per essere pronti per Tryngolza. Ciò include lavorare in stretta collaborazione con la funzione Launch Excellence e altri team CEERJ per garantire che le modalità di lavoro, i processi, la governance, l'assetto organizzativo e le capacità tra le funzioni siano adatti allo scopo di supportare il prodotto come parte del portafoglio CEERJ. Oltre alle responsabilità del PMO e della preparazione organizzativa, il ruolo fornisce un supporto flessibile e pratico attraverso la piattaforma commerciale CEERJ, compreso l'intervento temporaneo per supportare le sotto-funzioni in caso di capacità o lacune di capacità (ad esempio copertura del congedo di maternità). Questa posizione è adatta per un professionista di medio livello e ad alto potenziale con esperienza di consulenza e analisi, forti capacità di esecuzione, elevata empatia e motivazione ad apprendere e operare in più aree commerciali in un ambiente complesso e a matrice. Responsabilità principali/principali: Proprietà del PMO commerciale CEERJ
• Stabilire, mantenere e migliorare continuamente il framework del PMO commerciale CEERJ
• Fornire una supervisione end-to-end delle iniziative di trasformazione ed esecuzione commerciale del CEERJ
• Consolidare priorità, tappe fondamentali, proprietà, rischi e interdipendenze tra le iniziative
• Consentire chiarezza sulla sequenza, focalizzazione e allocazione delle risorse attraverso la piattaforma commerciale
• Promuovere un follow-up disciplinato su azioni, decisioni e impegni concordati Preparazione organizzativa di Tryngolza (PMO regionale Responsabile)
• Agire come proprietario del PMO per la preparazione organizzativa del CEERJ per Tryngolza, con la responsabilità di coordinare e guidare la preparazione organizzativa tra le funzioni
• Progettare e gestire un quadro di preparazione strutturato che affronti: o modalità di lavoro o processi e governance o assetto e interfacce organizzative o preparazione di capacità e competenze
• Lavorare in stretta collaborazione con Launch Excellence e altre funzioni del CEERJ per allineare attività di preparazione, tempistiche e aspettative
• Garantire la comprensione condivisa di ruoli, responsabilità e dipendenze interfunzionali
• Identificare attività organizzative lacune, rischi e interdipendenze; guidare azioni di mitigazione e un'escalation tempestiva
• Monitorare e comunicare i progressi della preparazione attraverso informazioni chiare e pronte per i dirigenti Abilitazione esecutiva e follow-through strategico
• Agire come partner di esecuzione fidato per l'eccellenza commerciale e il direttore dell'esecuzione della strategia del CEERJ
• Supportare la focalizzazione dell'agenda, la definizione delle priorità e il follow-up sulle priorità strategiche e di trasformazione
• Preparare aggiornamenti strutturati, dashboard e materiali pronti per prendere decisioni per i forum di leadership
• Tradurre input complessi in implicazioni chiare e azioni consigliate Coordinamento interfunzionale e risoluzione costruttiva dei conflitti
• Interagire a stretto contatto con le sottofunzioni del CEERJ per garantire comprensione e allineamento condivisi
• Emergere disallineamenti o tensioni tempestivamente e supportare la risoluzione attraverso l'ascolto profondo e l'assunzione di prospettive
• Risolvere i conflitti facilitando compromessi equilibrati e compromessi pragmatici
• Promuovere un modo collaborativo e basato sulla fiducia di lavorare attraverso la piattaforma Fornitura flessibile e supporto delle capacità
• Supportare temporaneamente le sottofunzioni durante lacune di capacità o capacità
• Fornire supporto pratico analitico, di progetto o di coordinamento dove aggiunge il massimo valore
• Adattare l'ambito e concentrarsi dinamicamente in linea con le priorità del CEERJ Insights Discovery & Analytics
• Applicare una mentalità basata sui dati e orientata agli insight per identificare modelli, rischi e opportunità di miglioramento
• Utilizzare l'analisi per supportare la definizione delle priorità, il monitoraggio delle prestazioni e il processo decisionale
• Convertire gli insight in raccomandazioni pratiche e attuabili Esperienza di istruzione/apprendimento
• Laurea universitaria
• Preferibile esperienza pregressa in consulenza
• È richiesta esperienza nel settore farmaceutico/biotecnologico
• Esperienza nel supportare o guidare la trasformazione organizzativa, il cambiamento del modello operativo o programmi interfunzionali complessi
• Esperienza con l'analisi dei dati, generazione di insight o gestione delle prestazioni Abilità/conoscenze
• Forti capacità analitiche, strutturanti e di risoluzione dei problemi
• Capacità di sintetizzare la complessità in narrazioni chiare
• Forte capacità di gestione e influenza degli stakeholder
• Eccellente disciplina di esecuzione e follow-through Attributi personali
• Umile, empatico e un eccellente ascoltatore
• Risolve i conflitti attraverso una profonda comprensione di molteplici prospettive
• Curioso, flessibile e motivato ad apprendere in aree commerciali
• Elevata integrità, mentalità di proprietà e responsabilità Lingue
• Inglese fluente (scritto e parlato) Commenti aggiuntivi
• Disponibilità a adattarsi ad argomenti, progetti e aree funzionali
• Disponibilità a viaggiare secondo necessità Tutti i dipendenti Sobi devono dimostrare comportamenti in linea con i valori fondamentali di Sobi: cura, ambizione, urgenza, proprietà e partnership. Sei pronto per far parte del team Sobi? Unisciti a una cultura che consente a ogni persona di essere la persona che fa la differenza per le malattie rare. Perché unirsi a noi? Siamo un'azienda globale con oltre 1.900 dipendenti in più di 30 paesi e ci impegniamo a favore delle società in cui operiamo. Con un team di gestione altamente qualificato che dirige le nostre vittorie quotidiane e un consiglio di amministrazione con un track record stellare, siamo pronti ad affrontare le malattie, i disturbi e le avversità del mondo. Le nostre persone credono di avere il potere di avere un impatto positivo nella vita degli altri perché è esattamente ciò che facciamo qui. Se stai cercando una carriera che sfrutti i tuoi talenti in modo da rendere il mondo un posto migliore e più sano, potremmo avere un lavoro adatto a te. Sappiamo che i nostri dipendenti sono la nostra risorsa più preziosa e la nostra cultura lo trasmette. Offriamo un pacchetto di vantaggi competitivi, per sostenere la salute e la felicità del nostro personale. Cultura Sobi Noi di Sobi ci rifiutiamo di accettare lo status quo. Questo perché siamo stati testimoni in prima persona delle sfide che devono affrontare le persone affette da malattie rare e abbiamo utilizzato questa conoscenza per modellare la nostra attività per trovare nuovi modi per aiutarli. In qualità di azienda biofarmaceutica specializzata, ci dedichiamo alle malattie rare. E vediamo questa attenzione come un punto di forza. Trasformando efficacemente la nostra ricerca in trattamenti innovativi, contribuiamo a rendere la medicina più accessibile e ad aprire più possibilità ai pazienti e più opportunità a coloro che si prendono cura di loro. Questo è stato il nostro approccio fin dal primo giorno, ma sappiamo che non possiamo cambiare il mondo delle malattie rare da soli. Per raggiungere questo obiettivo sono necessarie forti collaborazioni con pazienti, partner e parti interessate lungo l’intera catena del valore. Insieme, definiamo come la nostra azienda può creare soluzioni che soddisfino le esigenze delle persone affette da malattie rare, facilitando al tempo stesso una crescita sostenibile. Un datore di lavoro che garantisce pari opportunità Sobi è un datore di lavoro che garantisce pari opportunità e proibisce discriminazioni e molestie di qualsiasi tipo. Tutte le decisioni sull'assunzione in Sobi si basano su esigenze aziendali, requisiti lavorativi e qualifiche individuali, indipendentemente da razza, colore, religione o convinzioni personali, origine nazionale, sociale o etnica, sesso (inclusa la gravidanza), età, disabilità fisica, mentale o sensoriale, stato HIV, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere, veterani e/o espressioni protetti, stato coniugale, unione civile o convivenza, servizio militare passato o presente, storia medica familiare o informazioni genetiche, stato familiare o genitoriale o qualsiasi altro stato o gruppo protetto dalle leggi o dai regolamenti nei luoghi in cui operiamo. Sobi è un datore di lavoro che promuove azioni positive e pari opportunità. Disabili/Veterani. Se sei una persona qualificata con disabilità o un veterano disabile, puoi richiedere una soluzione ragionevole se non sei in grado o hai capacità limitate di utilizzare o accedere a questo sito Web per presentare domanda per un posto vacante a causa della tua disabilità. Puoi richiedere sistemazioni ragionevoli inviando un'e-mail a talentacquisition@sobi.com.

Die Aufgabe besteht darin, den CEERJ-Initiativen in den Bereichen Launch Excellence, Commercial Excellence, Business Development & Alliance Management sowie Partnermärkte Struktur, Transparenz, Priorisierung und Nachverfolgung zu verleihen. Es unterstützt den CEERJ Commercial Excellence & Strategy Execution Director dabei, sicherzustellen, dass Prioritäten auf der gesamten Plattform klar sind, gegenseitige Abhängigkeiten aktiv verwaltet werden und vereinbarte Maßnahmen konsistent ausgeführt werden. Eine zentrale und ausdrückliche Verantwortung der Rolle besteht darin, die organisatorische Bereitschaft von CEERJ für Tryngolza zu leiten und voranzutreiben und als regionaler PMO-Eigentümer für die organisatorische und betriebliche Transformation zu fungieren, die erforderlich ist, um für Tryngolza bereit zu sein. Dazu gehört die enge Zusammenarbeit mit der Launch Excellence-Funktion und anderen CEERJ-Teams, um sicherzustellen, dass Arbeitsweisen, Prozesse, Governance, Organisationsaufbau und funktionsübergreifende Fähigkeiten zur Unterstützung des Produkts als Teil des CEERJ-Portfolios geeignet sind. Zusätzlich zu den PMO- und organisatorischen Bereitschaftsaufgaben bietet die Rolle flexible, praktische Unterstützung auf der gesamten CEERJ-Kommerziellplattform, einschließlich der vorübergehenden Unterstützung von Unterfunktionen im Falle von Kapazitäts- oder Fähigkeitslücken (z. B. Mutterschaftsurlaubsschutz). Diese Position eignet sich gut für einen Fachmann auf mittlerer Ebene mit hohem Potenzial, der über Beratungs- und Analyseerfahrung, ausgeprägte Ausführungsfähigkeiten, hohes Einfühlungsvermögen und die Motivation verfügt, in mehreren kaufmännischen Bereichen in einem komplexen, Matrixumfeld zu lernen und zu agieren. • Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Launch Excellence und anderen CEERJ-Funktionen, um Bereitschaftsaktivitäten, Zeitpläne und Erwartungen aufeinander abzustimmen.

• Fungieren Sie als vertrauenswürdiger Ausführungspartner des CEERJ Commercial Excellence & Strategy Execution Director.
• Unterstützen Sie die Agendafokussierung, Priorisierung und Nachverfolgung von Strategie- und Transformationsprioritäten.

• Arbeiten Sie eng mit den CEERJ-Unterfunktionen zusammen, um ein gemeinsames Verständnis und eine gemeinsame Abstimmung sicherzustellen.

• Umfang und Fokus dynamisch im Einklang mit den CEERJ-Prioritäten anpassen. Insights Discovery & Analytics

• Erfahrung mit Datenanalyse, Erkenntnisgewinnung oder Leistungsmanagement Fähigkeiten/Wissen

• Bereitschaft zu Reisen nach Bedarf Alle Sobi-Mitarbeiter müssen Verhaltensweisen zeigen, die den Grundwerten von Sobi entsprechen: Fürsorge, Ehrgeiz, Dringlichkeit, Eigenverantwortung und Partnerschaft. Sind Sie bereit, Teil des Sobi-Teams zu sein? Werden Sie Teil einer Kultur, die jeden Menschen dazu befähigt, die Person zu sein, die bei seltenen Krankheiten einen Unterschied macht. Warum sollten Sie bei uns mitmachen? Wir sind ein globales Unternehmen mit über 1.900 Mitarbeitern in mehr als 30 Ländern und engagieren uns für die Gesellschaften, in denen wir tätig sind. Mit einem hochqualifizierten Managementteam, das unsere täglichen Erfolge steuert, und einem Vorstand mit einer herausragenden Erfolgsbilanz sind wir bereit, uns den Krankheiten, Leiden und Widrigkeiten der Welt zu stellen. Unsere Mitarbeiter glauben, dass sie die Macht haben, das Leben anderer positiv zu beeinflussen, denn genau das tun wir hier. Wenn Sie auf der Suche nach einer Karriere sind, die Ihre Talente auf eine Art und Weise nutzt, die die Welt zu einem besseren und gesünderen Ort macht, haben wir vielleicht genau den richtigen Job für Sie. Wir wissen, dass unsere Mitarbeiter unser wertvollstes Kapital sind, und unsere Kultur vermittelt dies. Wir bieten ein wettbewerbsfähiges Leistungspaket, um die Gesundheit und Zufriedenheit unserer Mitarbeiter zu unterstützen. Sobi-Kultur Bei Sobi weigern wir uns, den Status quo zu akzeptieren. Dies liegt daran, dass wir die Herausforderungen, vor denen die von seltenen Krankheiten Betroffenen stehen, aus erster Hand miterlebt haben und dieses Wissen genutzt haben, um unser Unternehmen so zu gestalten, dass wir neue Wege finden, ihnen zu helfen. Als spezialisiertes biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen widmen wir uns seltenen Krankheiten. Und wir sehen diesen Fokus als Stärke. Indem wir unsere Forschung effektiv in bahnbrechende Behandlungen umsetzen, tragen wir dazu bei, die Medizin zugänglicher zu machen und mehr Möglichkeiten für Patienten und mehr Möglichkeiten für diejenigen zu eröffnen, die sie betreuen. Dies ist seit dem ersten Tag unser Ansatz, aber wir wissen, dass wir die Welt der seltenen Krankheiten nicht alleine verändern können. Um dies zu erreichen, sind starke Partnerschaften mit Patienten, Partnern und Interessengruppen entlang der gesamten Wertschöpfungskette erforderlich. Gemeinsam definieren wir, wie unser Unternehmen Lösungen schaffen kann, die den Bedürfnissen der von seltenen Krankheiten Betroffenen gerecht werden und gleichzeitig nachhaltiges Wachstum ermöglichen.
